decks.name: Classic
decks.blurb: Everyday opinions to break the ice.
statements: Pineapple belongs on pizza.
Cereal counts as a soup.
A hot dog is a sandwich.
Mornings are better than nights.
Texting is better than calling.
Cold weather beats hot weather.
Cake is overrated.
Coffee is better than tea.
Dogs are better than cats.
Reading the book is better than watching the movie.
Sweet popcorn beats salty popcorn.
The window seat is the best seat.
Showers are better in the morning.
Board games are more fun than video games.
Working from home beats the office.
Sandals are an acceptable winter shoe.
Breakfast food is the best food at any hour.
It is fine to put ice in milk.
Toilet paper should hang over the roll, not under.
Quiet weekends beat busy weekends.
A messy desk means a creative mind.
Walking is better than driving for short trips.
Leftovers taste better the next day.
Socks with sandals look good.
The aisle seat is better than the window seat.
Audiobooks count as reading.
Spicy food is overrated.
Tap water tastes better than bottled water.
Surprise parties are a great idea.
Mint and chocolate are a perfect pair.

decks.name: Food Fight
statements: Ketchup belongs in the fridge, not the cupboard.
Well-done steak is a crime.
Soup is a perfectly good dinner.
Crunchy peanut butter beats smooth.
A burrito is just a wrap with confidence.
Ranch goes on everything.
Brunch is the best meal of the week.
Dark chocolate is better than milk chocolate.
Fries should be dipped, not eaten plain.
Cold pizza is a legitimate breakfast.
Olives ruin a good dish.
A wrap is healthier than a sandwich.
Ice cream is fine in winter.
Garlic improves every savory dish.
Burnt toast is better than soggy toast.
Cheese belongs on apple pie.
Sparkling water is better than still.
Mushrooms have no business on a pizza.
Eating dessert first is a smart move.
Hot sauce should come with everything.

decks.name: Tech and Life
statements: Phones should be left at the table during dinner.
Light mode is better than dark mode.
A physical book beats an e-reader.
Replying with a voice note is better than typing.
Notifications should be off by default.
Tabs are better than spaces.
Inbox zero is worth the effort.
A loud keyboard is a great keyboard.
Smartwatches are more useful than regular watches.
Paper maps are better than navigation apps.
Group chats should have a strict topic.
It is fine to read the ending of a story first.
Saving every photo you take is a good idea.
A clean home screen is a sign of a clear mind.
Read receipts should always be on.
Wired headphones are better than wireless.
A to-do list on paper beats an app.
Streaming is better than owning movies.
Closing every browser tab at night feels great.
A password should be a long sentence, not a short code.

decks.name: Pop Culture
statements: The sequel is usually better than the original.
Subtitles make any movie better.
A playlist on shuffle is the only right way to listen.
Re-watching a favorite beats trying something new.
The villain is usually more interesting than the hero.
A short film can be better than a long one.
Live music always beats a recording.
Spoilers do not actually ruin anything.
A book series should always have a clear ending.
Background music makes work better.
The first season is always the best season.
Comedies are harder to make than dramas.
A great soundtrack can save a weak movie.
Dancing in public is a good idea.
Karaoke is fun even when you cannot sing.
Old cartoons are better than new ones.
Reading reviews before watching ruins the fun.
Every story is better with a narrator.
Musicals are underrated.
A cliffhanger ending is a great ending.

decks.name: On the Road
decks.blurb: Trips, packing, and the great outdoors.
statements: Packing light is always the right call.
A road trip beats a flight.
Mountains are better than beaches.
Planning every day of a trip is the best way to travel.
Camping is genuinely relaxing.
Travelling alone is better than in a group.
A window seat is worth the wait for the bathroom.
The journey is better than the destination.
Souvenirs are a waste of money.
An early flight is better than a late one.
Trying new food is the best part of a trip.
A long train ride is the best way to see a place.
Getting lost on purpose makes a better trip.
Staying in one city beats hopping between many.
A backpack beats a suitcase.
Tourist spots are popular for a good reason.
Sunrise is worth waking up early for.
A staycation can be just as good as a trip.
Maps should be studied before you arrive.
The best trips are the unplanned ones.
